steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The system consists of a downward-opening parabola and a horizontal line at $y = 6$. The solutions are the $x$-values at the intersection points of the two curves.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$x = 4$': The parabola crosses $y = 6$ at $x = 4$ (left intersection, readable from the grid). ✓
  • Choice B: Not an $x$-coordinate where the curves intersect. ✗
  • Choice C: Not an $x$-coordinate where the curves intersect. ✗
  • Choice D: Not an $x$-coordinate where the curves intersect. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
